title: Valid Palindrome
description:
A phrase is a **palindrome** if, after lowercasing and removing all
non-alphanumeric characters, it reads the same forward and backward. Given a
string `s`, return `true` if it is a palindrome.

examples.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

tests.name: classic phrase
args: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

solutions.summary: Walk inward from both ends, skipping non-alphanumeric characters.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `true` return.

steps.title: Place a pointer at each end
steps.explanation: `left` starts at the front and `right` at the back.

code.python:
def is_palindrome(s):
    left = 0
    right = len(s) - 1
    return True

steps.title: Skip non-alphanumeric characters
steps.explanation: Move the pointers past anything that is not a letter or digit.

steps.title: Compare the two characters
steps.explanation: When both sides are alphanumeric, a case-insensitive mismatch means it is not a palindrome.

steps.title: Step both pointers inward
steps.explanation: On a match, advance both ends toward the middle.

solutions.summary: Strip the string down, then compare it with its reverse.

steps.title: Start from the function shape
steps.explanation: Begin with the signature and a default `true` return.

steps.title: Keep only lowercase alphanumerics
steps.explanation: Filter out everything else so we compare just the meaningful characters.

steps.title: Compare with the reverse
steps.explanation: A palindrome equals itself read backward.
